bisect.help: binary search through commits to find a bug, with safety guards
bisect.name: bisect

checkout.help: checkout a branch or ref with working-tree safety guards
checkout.name: checkout

cherry-pick.help: cherry-pick one or more commits onto HEAD with safety guards
cherry-pick.name: cherry-pick

args.help: files to commit (supports hunk specs: file.go:1,3)
args.name: files

flags.help: commit message line; can be repeated to build multi-line messages
flags.name: m

flags.help: read the full commit message body from a file instead of --m flags
flags.name: F

flags.help: commit the staged files onto a different branch without switching to it
flags.name: branch

flags.help: amend the current HEAD commit by replacing it with updated content
flags.name: amend

flags.help: allow creating a commit even when no files have been changed
flags.name: allow-empty

flags.help: add a key-value trailer line to the commit message (repeatable)
flags.name: trailer

commit.help: stage and commit specified files in a single atomic operation
commit.name: commit

flags.help: run all health checks and report results without fixing any issues
flags.name: diagnose

flags.help: run all health checks and automatically repair any issues found
flags.name: fix

flags.help: remove all safegit hooks and metadata from this repository entirely
flags.name: uninstall

doctor.help: run diagnostic health checks on the repository and optionally repair issues
doctor.name: doctor

merge.help: merge a branch into HEAD with working-tree safety guards
merge.name: merge

args.help: name of the remote repository to pull from (defaults to origin)
args.name: remote

args.help: name of the remote branch to fetch and merge into the current branch
args.name: branch

flags.help: fast-forward only; abort with an error if a merge commit is required
flags.name: ff-only

flags.help: fast-forward when possible, otherwise create a merge commit automatically
flags.name: ff

flags.help: always create a merge commit even when fast-forward is possible
flags.name: no-ff

pull.help: fetch from remote and merge, defaulting to fast-forward-only mode
pull.name: pull

args.help: name of the remote repository to push to (defaults to origin)
args.name: remote

flags.help: skip running pre-pre-push hook scripts before pushing to remote
flags.name: no-pre-pre-push

flags.help: force push using --force-with-lease to prevent overwriting others' work
flags.name: force-with-lease

flags.help: push only the current HEAD branch to the remote, ignoring other refs
flags.name: only-head

flags.help: push all local branches to the remote, ignoring tags and other refs
flags.name: only-branches

flags.help: push all local tags to the remote without pushing any branches
flags.name: only-tags

flags.help: push all local branches and all tags to the remote in one operation
flags.name: both-branches-and-tags

push.help: push refs to remote with pre-pre-push hooks and automatic retry
push.name: push

rebase.help: rebase current branch onto upstream with safety guards
rebase.name: rebase

flags.help: redo across all sessions by ignoring the session ID ownership check
flags.name: bypass-session

redo.help: restore the commit that undo removed, as a one-shot counterpart to undo
redo.name: redo

reset.help: reset HEAD with guards that prevent accidental --hard data loss
reset.name: reset

revert.help: revert one or more commits creating inverse patches, with safety guards
revert.name: revert

flags.help: current author or committer display name to search for and replace
flags.name: old-name

flags.help: new display name to substitute wherever the old name is found in history
flags.name: new-name

flags.help: current author or committer email address to search for and replace
flags.name: old-email

flags.help: new email address to substitute wherever the old email is found in history
flags.name: new-email

rewrite-author.help: rewrite author and committer name or email across all commit history
rewrite-author.name: rewrite-author

flags.help: undo across all sessions by ignoring the session ID ownership check
flags.name: bypass-session

undo.help: reverse the last commit, amend, or reword operation using the oplog
undo.name: undo

args.help: the ref name (e.g. refs/heads/main) whose stale .lock file to remove
args.name: ref

unlock.help: release a stale .lock file left behind by a crashed git process
unlock.name: unlock

version.help: print safegit version, Go runtime version, and git version
version.name: version

global_flags.help: suppress all informational output, only showing errors and results
global_flags.name: quiet

global_flags.help: enable verbose output with detailed progress and diagnostic info
global_flags.name: verbose

global_flags.help: preview what would happen without writing any changes to disk
global_flags.name: dry-run

global_flags.help: automatically confirm all interactive prompts without asking
global_flags.name: yes

global_flags.help: path to a custom safegit config file instead of the default location
global_flags.name: config

global_flags.help: emit machine-readable JSON output to stdout instead of human text
global_flags.name: json

args.help: the configuration key whose current value should be retrieved
args.name: key

get.help: get the current value of a single configuration key from the .git/safegit/config.json file, printing the raw value to stdout so it can be captured by scripts or used in automation pipelines
get.name: get

args.help: the configuration key to set to the specified value in config.json
args.name: key

args.help: the new value to assign to the specified configuration key
args.name: value

set.help: set a configuration key to a new value in the .git/safegit/config.json file, creating the file if it does not exist yet, and persisting the change for all future safegit invocations in this repository
set.name: set

show.help: show all configuration values currently in effect for this repository, including built-in defaults and any user overrides from the .git/safegit/config.json file, printed as key-value pairs to stdout for inspection and debugging purposes
show.name: show

config.help: show, get, or set safegit configuration key-value pairs
config.name: config

args.help: filesystem path to the hook script file to install into safegit
args.name: path

install.help: install a pre-pre-push hook by copying a script file into the .git/safegit/hooks directory, making it executable, and registering it so that safegit push will run it before any network I/O occurs
install.name: install

list.help: list all pre-pre-push hooks currently installed in the .git/safegit/hooks directory, showing each hook name, file path, and whether it is executable, so you can audit which checks run before every push
list.name: list

args.help: name of a specific hook to run; omit to run all installed hooks
args.name: name

run.help: run all installed pre-pre-push hooks (or a single named hook) immediately without performing an actual push, so you can verify that all configured hooks pass before committing to a real push operation
run.name: run

hook.help: manage pre-pre-push hook scripts that run before every push
hook.name: hook

args.help: repository-relative path to the file that should be scrubbed from history
args.name: file

flags.help: first commit hash to include when rewriting history (default: root commit)
flags.name: from

flags.help: mandatory audit trail message explaining why this scrub operation is needed
flags.name: reason

file.help: replace or remove a specific file across all commits in the repository history, rewriting each affected commit tree to either substitute the file contents with a sanitized version or delete the file entirely from every historical snapshot
file.name: file

flags.help: regular expression pattern to search for across all blobs in history
flags.name: pattern

flags.help: mandatory audit trail message explaining why this scrub operation is needed
flags.name: reason

flags.help: glob pattern limiting which file paths are searched (e.g. '*.env', 'config/**')
flags.name: scope

flags.help: literal string to substitute for each regex match found in history
flags.name: replace

flags.help: replace matches with random printable ASCII of same length
flags.name: mangle

flags.help: first commit hash to include when rewriting history (default: root commit)
flags.name: from

flags.help: rewrite all commits from the root of the repository to HEAD
flags.name: entire-history

match.help: replace all occurrences of a regex pattern across every blob in the repository history, rewriting commit trees to substitute matched text with a replacement string so that sensitive values like secrets and credentials are permanently removed from all historical snapshots
match.name: match

scrub.help: surgically rewrite git history to remove or replace sensitive content such as secrets, credentials, and private data from all commits, trees, and blobs in the repository
scrub.name: scrub
